User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the FinTrack Automation application, holding their personal information and settings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

Account.title: Account
Account.description: Represents a financial account (e.g., bank account, credit card) belonging to a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Account entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this account. (Relationship: User 1:N Account)

name.description: A user-defined name for the account (e.g., 'Checking Account', 'Savings').

type.description: The type of financial account (e.g., 'Bank', 'Credit Card', 'Cash', 'Investment').

currency.description: The currency code for the account (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

balance.description: The current balance of the account.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the account was last updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a category for financial transactions (e.g., 'Groceries', 'Salary', 'Rent').

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this category. (Relationship: User 1:N Category)

type.description: The type of financial flow this category represents (e.g., 'Expense', 'Income', 'Transfer').

description.description: A brief description of the category.

parentCategoryId.description: Reference to a parent category for hierarchical categorization. (Relationship: Category 1:N Category - self-referencing)

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Represents a single financial transaction, such as an expense or income.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this transaction. (Relationship: User 1:N Transaction)

accountId.description: Reference to the Account where this transaction occurred. (Relationship: Account 1:N Transaction)

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category assigned to this transaction. (Relationship: Category 1:N Transaction)

description.description: A description or payee/payer for the transaction.

amount.description: The monetary amount of the transaction. Positive for income, negative for expense.

date.description: The date when the transaction occurred.

isAutomated.description: Indicates if the transaction was automatically entered or categorized.

status.description: The current status of the transaction (e.g., 'cleared', 'pending', 'reconciled').

sourceDocumentId.description: Reference to the Document from which this transaction was extracted. (Relationship: Document 1:N Transaction)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction record was last updated.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents an uploaded financial document (e.g., receipt, invoice, bank statement) for OCR processing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who uploaded this document. (Relationship: User 1:N Document)

fileName.description: The original file name of the uploaded document.

fileUrl.description: The URL where the document file is stored.

uploadDate.description: The date when the document was uploaded.

extractedText.description: The full text content extracted from the document using OCR.

status.description: The processing status of the document (e.g., 'uploaded', 'processing', 'processed', 'error').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the document record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the document record was last updated.

Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a customer (individual or company) to whom invoices are issued.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Customer entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who manages this customer. (Relationship: User 1:N Customer)

name.description: The full name or company name of the customer.

email.description: The primary email address for contacting the customer.

address.description: The billing address of the customer.

phone.description: The phone number of the customer.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the customer record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the customer record was last updated.

Invoice.title: Invoice
Invoice.description: Represents an invoice created, sent, and tracked by the user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Invoice entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who created this invoice. (Relationship: User 1:N Invoice)

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer to whom this invoice is issued. (Relationship: Customer 1:N Invoice)

invoiceNumber.description: A unique, sequential identifier for the invoice.

issueDate.description: The date when the invoice was issued.

dueDate.description: The date when the invoice payment is due.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the invoice.

currency.description: The currency code for the invoice (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

status.description: The current status of the invoice (e.g., 'draft', 'sent', 'paid', 'overdue', 'void').

paymentDate.description: The date when payment for the invoice was received.

paymentTransactionId.description: Reference to the Transaction representing the payment received for this invoice. (Relationship: Transaction 1:N Invoice Payment)

notes.description: Any additional notes or terms for the invoice.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the invoice record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the invoice record was last updated.

InvoiceLineItem.title: InvoiceLineItem
InvoiceLineItem.description: Represents a single line item within an invoice, detailing a product or service.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InvoiceLineItem entity.

invoiceId.description: Reference to the Invoice this line item belongs to. (Relationship: Invoice 1:N InvoiceLineItem)

description.description: A description of the product or service being billed.

quantity.description: The quantity of the item or service.

unitPrice.description: The price per unit of the item or service.

amount.description: The total amount for this line item (quantity * unitPrice).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the invoice line item record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the invoice line item record was last updated.

definition.description: Root collection for user profiles. Each document represents a single user and is owned by the user whose UID matches {userId}.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Collection for financial accounts owned by a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the owning user.

params.name: accountId
params.description: The unique ID of the financial account.

definition.description: Collection for transaction categories created and owned by a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the owning user.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique ID of the transaction category.

definition.description: Collection for financial transactions recorded by a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the owning user.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique ID of the financial transaction.

definition.description: Collection for financial documents uploaded by a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the owning user.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique ID of the uploaded document.

definition.description: Collection for customer records managed by a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the owning user.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique ID of the customer record.

definition.description: Collection for invoices created and managed by a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the owning user.

params.name: invoiceId
params.description: The unique ID of the invoice.

definition.description: Subcollection for line items belonging to a specific invoice. CRITICAL: Requires denormalized 'userId' from the parent Invoice and 'invoiceId' in each document for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the owning user (denormalized from parent invoice).

params.name: invoiceId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent invoice (denormalized into line item document).

params.name: lineItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the invoice line item.

firestore.reasoning:
The FinTrack Automation Firestore structure prioritizes robust security, scalability, and debuggability by adhering strictly to the core design principles.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) & Denormalization:**
Every collection path, especially for user-specific data, is designed with path-based ownership, leveraging the `/users/{userId}/` pattern. For entities like `Account`, `Category`, `Transaction`, `Document`, `Customer`, and `Invoice`, the `userId` is explicitly included in the document schema and is also part of the document's path (`/users/{userId}/<collection>/<docId>`). This ensures that authorization rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, eliminating the need for `get()` calls to parent documents to determine ownership. This design makes atomic operations like creating a user and their initial settings much simpler and more secure.

For `InvoiceLineItem`, which originally lacked a `userId` in its schema but resides as a subcollection under an Invoice, the `userId` from its parent `Invoice` (and thus, the owning `User`) MUST be denormalized and stored directly within each `InvoiceLineItem` document. This allows a rule matching `/users/{userId}/invoices/{invoiceId}/lineItems/{lineItemId}` to directly verify `request.auth.uid == userId` without an expensive and rule-breaking `get()` operation on the parent `Invoice` document. The `invoiceId` should also be denormalized into `InvoiceLineItem` to simplify rule logic and querying.

**Structural Segregation & QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
All collections are designed to have a homogeneous security posture. For instance, all documents within `/users/{userId}/accounts` are owned by and accessible only to `{userId}`. This prevents mixing data with different access requirements in the same collection, which would necessitate complex and error-prone `where` clauses or conditional logic in security rules. By embedding the `userId` in the path and the document, `list` operations (QAPs) are inherently secure. A query to `/users/{request.auth.uid}/accounts` will only ever return accounts owned by the authenticated user, and the security rules will enforce this path-based ownership, preventing data leakage or unauthorized access during list operations without acting as filters.

**Access Modeling & Consistency:**
*   **Private Data & Hierarchical Paths:** All user-centric data (User, Account, Category, Transaction, Document, Customer, Invoice) follows the `/users/{userId}/<collection>/<docId>` pattern, establishing clear, private ownership. This makes writing concise and correct security rules for CRUD operations trivial.
*   **Nested Data:** `InvoiceLineItem` continues this hierarchy by being nested under `Invoice` (`/users/{userId}/invoices/{invoiceId}/lineItems/{lineItemId}`), but with the critical denormalization of `userId` and `invoiceId` into each line item document to maintain authorization independence.

This architecture ensures that security rules are straightforward, explicit, and easy to debug, primarily relying on path matching and direct field comparisons, significantly reducing the surface area for security vulnerabilities.
